label: Write Rules
description: Designing instructions, constraints, or algorithms that define how a work can be generated.

label: Introduce Variation
description: Allowing chance, randomness, noise, mutation, or probability to alter a system's output.

label: Execute Systems
description: Delegating the performance or production of a work to a machine, program, plotter, or robot.

label: Evolve Outcomes
description: Creating systems whose forms develop through emergence, feedback, interaction, or simulated evolution.

label: Negotiate Authorship
description: Questioning how authorship is distributed among artists, datasets, machines, collaborators, and audiences.

label: John Cage
description: Used chance operations and indeterminacy to reduce direct authorial control.

label: Sol LeWitt
description: Made instructions and systems central to the production of artworks.

label: Vera Molnár
description: Used algorithms and controlled variation in pioneering computer-generated art.

label: Georg Nees
description: Used programmed computers and plotters to produce early algorithmic drawings.

label: Frieder Nake
description: Developed early computer graphics through mathematical rules and plotter execution.

label: Harold Cohen
description: Developed AARON as a long-term collaboration between artistic knowledge and software.

label: Karl Sims
description: Used genetic algorithms and artificial evolution to generate images and virtual organisms.

label: Sommerer & Mignonneau
description: Created interactive artificial-life systems shaped by participant behavior.

label: Casey Reas
description: Builds rule-based processes whose visual forms emerge while software runs.

label: Anna Ridler
description: Builds handmade datasets and uses machine learning to expose the politics of classification.

label: Sougwen Chung
description: Collaborates with drawing robots trained on the artist's own gestures.

label: Entangled Others
description: Uses computation, machine learning, and biological metaphors to explore nonhuman forms and co-creation.

label: Instruction
description: A transferable procedure that allows a work to be performed by someone or something else.

label: Constraint
description: A boundary that limits possibilities while structuring a generative space.

label: Algorithm
description: A formal sequence of operations that transforms inputs into outputs.

label: Chance
description: An intentional opening for unpredictable events.

label: Randomness
description: Computational or procedural unpredictability used to diversify results.

label: Mutation
description: A change introduced into inherited or repeated information.

label: Emergence
description: Complex form or behavior arising from interactions among simple rules.

label: Artificial Life
description: The simulation or construction of lifelike behavior through computational systems.

label: Dataset
description: A selected and organized body of examples that shapes what a learning system can produce.

label: Authorship
description: The attribution of creative agency, responsibility, and ownership.

label: Human–Machine Collaboration
description: A creative relation in which human and computational agents influence the result.

label: Plotter
description: A machine that materializes programmed instructions as lines on paper or other surfaces.

label: AARON
description: Harold Cohen's artmaking software, executed through screens, plotters, and painting machines.

label: Genetic Algorithm
description: A search method that evolves candidate forms through selection, mutation, and recombination.

label: Neural Network
description: A learning system trained to model patterns in data.

label: GAN
description: A generative model in which two neural networks compete during training.

label: Robotic Drawing
description: The use of programmable machines to perform drawing gestures.

label: Wall Drawings
description: Instruction-based works whose final appearance depends on their execution.

label: Interruptions
description: Vera Molnár's systematic grid works in which controlled disruptions create variation.

label: Evolved Virtual Creatures
description: Karl Sims's simulated organisms evolved for movement and behavior.

label: A-Volve
description: An interactive environment in which artificial creatures develop through participant interaction.

label: Process
description: Casey Reas's software-based works generated by interacting instructions.

label: Mosaic Virus
description: Anna Ridler's project using a hand-built tulip dataset and machine learning.

label: Drawing Operations
description: Sougwen Chung's ongoing human–robot drawing collaborations.

label: Self-contained
description: Entangled Others' project combining generative mutation, neural networks, synthetic DNA, and sculptural materialization.
